title: PEH Data Platform – User Guidance
section: What is the PEH Data Platform
text:
What is the PEH Data Platform
The Personal Exposure and Health (PEH) Data Platform is a secure infrastructure developed and managed by VITO to facilitate the collection, harmonized storage, and controlled access of pseudonymised or anonymised single measurement data from population studies. The platform enables Supplying Data Controllers (SDCs) to deposit data, and Receiving Data Controllers (RDCs) to access approved subsets of PEH data for re-use, in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Protocol and all applicable legal and ethical requirements. The PEH Data Platform supports transparent data governance, ensures data protection and traceability, and provides operational services for data harmonisation, data processing, quality control, and access management.
https://peh.vito.be/

title: Data requirements
text:
This page describes the data requirements for using the PEH Data Platform.
It explains: - which data structures are expected - the role of templates and codebooks - how deviations from the specification are handled
This information is essential for correct and consistent use of the platform.

title: Data requirements
text:
This page describes the data requirements for using the PEH Data Platform.
It explains: - which data structures are expected - the role of templates and codebooks - how deviations from the specification are handled
This information is essential for correct and consistent use of the platform.

title: Data requirements
text:
General principles
The PEH Data Platform assumes that data providers:

work with predefined templates
follow agreed codebooks
take responsibility for data correctness and completeness

The platform supports inspection and harmonisation, but does not enforce or modify data.

title: Data requirements
text:
Templates

What is a template?
A template defines: - the expected structure of the dataset - variable names - basic formatting rules (e.g. one variable per column)
Templates are typically provided by the project or coordinating body.


Template expectations
Input data should: - match the template structure - contain all required variables - use consistent naming and formatting
Missing variables or additional, non‑specified variables are reported by the platform.

title: Data requirements
text:
Codebooks

Role of codebooks
Codebooks describe the meaning and coding of variables, including: - variable definitions - allowed values or value domains - units and categories where applicable
They form the reference against which input data are inspected.


Allowed values
Where allowed values are specified: - the platform checks whether data values fall within the defined domain - deviations are flagged, not corrected






Note



The platform does not harmonise or recode values automatically.

title: Data requirements
text:
Relationship between templates and codebooks
Templates and codebooks are complementary:

Templates define structure
Codebooks define semantics

Correct use of the PEH Data Platform requires alignment with both.

title: Data requirements
text:
Handling deviations
The following situations may occur:

missing required variables
additional variables not defined in the codebook
values outside the allowed domain

In all cases: - deviations are reported or summarised - data are not altered - responsibility remains with the data provider

title: Data requirements
text:
Responsibilities of data providers






Important



Use of the PEH Data Platform does not shift responsibility for data quality.
Data providers remain responsible for: - correctness of their data - alignment with project specifications - ethical and regulatory compliance


The platform is a supporting tool, not a validation authority.

title: Data requirements
text:
Where to find specifications
Project‑specific templates, codebooks and metadata specifications are typically provided: - via the project repository - as supplementary documentation - or through dedicated data coordination teams
Always ensure you are using the correct and most recent version of the specifications.

text:
Browse and download information on available studies
Explore and download codebooks for each study
Consult the procedure and documents to request data from the Personal Exposure and Health Data Platform

title: Overview
text:
PEH Data Platform
The Personal Exposure and Health (PEH) Data Platform is a secure infrastructure developed and managed by VITO to facilitate the collection, harmonized storage, and controlled access of pseudonymised or anonymised single measurement data from population studies. The platform enables Supplying Data Controllers (SDCs) to deposit data, and Receiving Data Controllers (RDCs) to access approved subsets of PEH data for re-use, in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Protocol and all applicable legal and ethical requirements. The PEH Data Platform supports transparent data governance, ensures data protection and traceability, and provides operational services for data harmonisation, data processing, quality control, and access management.
https://peh.vito.be/

title: Overview
text:
Contact
For questions: PEHDataPlatform@vito.be

title: Account
text:
Register for an account
To apply for an account to the PEH Data Platform, go to https://peh.vito.be/ and follow the instructions.
Important note:
Providing data to the PEH Data Platform and getting access to data from the PEH Data Platform is only possible upon signature of the Protocol On the management and use of the Personal Exposure and Health Data Platform

title: Data requests
text:
Access to individual-level data stored in the Personal Exposure and Health (PEH) Data Platform is granted on request and subject to approval.



To request access to PEH data:

Select studies and variables in this application

Download and complete the annex files
Submit the Data Access Request Form

Access is granted only after approval and signature of the Protocol on the management and use of the Personal Exposure and Health Data Platform.




Data access requests can be submitted by a Receiving Data Controller (RDC) for research projects in the public interest, in line with the objectives of the PEH Data Platform.
If multiple RDCs are involved in the same research project, and they require different subsets of data,
each RDC must submit a separate request, specifying: - their own research questions, and - the specific data requested.







The RDC submits a Data Access Request Form via Microsoft Forms.
The request is reviewed by the Data Request Access Committee (DRAC), including a data minimisation check.
The original data providers (Supplying Data Controllers – SDCs) may object to the request.
If approved, access is granted:

to the requested subset of PEH data (studies and variables),
filtered to studies for which no SDC objection was raised, and
only after signature of the Protocol by the RDC
(https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19232226).

Approved data extracts are made available through the PEH Data Platform:
https://peh.vito.be/






The Data Access Request Form collects the following information.



Main project name (acronym and full name)
Research project title
Date of submission





Scientific background
Objective and specific research questions





Study design
Target population
(e.g. age groups, sex, inclusion/exclusion criteria)





Requested datasets and studies
Requested variables


📌 Requested data must be specified using the official annex files generated via this application.





Statistical methods and analytical models
Sensitivity or pooled analyses (if applicable)
Requirements for data comparability





Explicit declaration that only data strictly necessary to answer the research questions are requested





Responsible person and institution
Contact details
Data analysts and (if applicable) external data processors
Planned study start and end dates
Publication intentions






To complete a Data Access Request, two annex files are required.
Both must be downloaded from this application.



Download the Annex – Studies file(s) from Studies.
For each study you request: - set request_stud_1isYes = 1
✅ All studies marked with request_stud_1isYes = 1 are included in the request.




Download the Annex – Variables file(s) from the Codebooks
For each variable you request: - set request_var_1isYes = 1
✅ All variables marked with request_var_1isYes = 1 are included in the request.
⚠️ Only annex files generated and completed via the Studies and Codebooks sections of this application are accepted.






Complete the Data Access Request Form via Microsoft Forms:
👉 https://forms.office.com/e/cvYykd6Q3S
To prepare your submission, you may optionally download the Data Access Request preparation template (Word document) This document contains the questions from the form and can be used to draft and review your answers before submission.
Download and complete the required annex Excel files via this application.
Send the completed annex files by e‑mail to:
PEHDataPlatform@vito.be

❗ Your request will not be processed until the annex files have been received.
To avoid requesting data for research questions that have already been explored, you can download an overview of publications based on data available through the PEH platform here.




Requests may involve sensitive personal data, including health data.
All processing must comply with applicable national and EU legal and ethical requirements.
All data requests are subject to the
Protocol on the management and use of the Personal Exposure and Health Data Platform.
Reference
Protocol on the management and use of the Personal Exposure and Health Data Platform.
Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19232226




For questions regarding data availability or the data access procedure, please contact:
PEHDataPlatform@vito.be

title: Overview
text:
This user guide describes how to use the PEH Data Platform in a correct and responsible way.
It focuses on: - required input structures - supported checks and inspections - interpretation of outputs - known limitations

title: Overview
text:
This user guide describes how to use the PEH Data Platform in a correct and responsible way.
It focuses on: - required input structures - supported checks and inspections - interpretation of outputs - known limitations

title: Overview
text:
Conceptual workflow
Use of the PEH Data Platform typically follows these steps:

Data are prepared according to an agreed PEH template or codebook

Data are loaded into the platform for inspection

Structure, variables and value domains are reviewed

Outputs are used to support further data curation or reporting

The platform supports the process, but does not replace expert judgement.

title: Overview
text:
Inputs
Input data are expected to be:

Structured in tabular form
Aligned with predefined variable names
Coded using specified allowed values where applicable

Deviation from the specification is reported, not automatically corrected.

title: Overview
text:
Outputs
Depending on the configuration, the platform may provide:

Structured overview tables
Summaries of variables and value domains
Diagnostic messages or warnings

Outputs should always be reviewed in the context of the original data and documentation.

title: Overview
text:
Limitations and responsibilities






Important



The PEH Data Platform is a supporting tool.
It does not: - guarantee regulatory or ethical compliance - detect conceptual errors in study design - replace formal data submission checks


Responsibility for data quality remains with the data provider.

title: Protocol
text:
Protocol On the management and use of the Personal Exposure and Health Data Platform
The Personal Exposure and Health Data Platform (“PEH Data Platform”) has been developed at VITO in order to give access and to share single measurement Personal Exposure and Health data (“PEH data”), most commonly originating from population studies, in the highest possible resolution to researchers and other stakeholders.
It is the intention of the Supplying Data Controllers of the data included in this platform, to make the data on this data platform further available to stakeholders according to the terms and conditions of this protocol and the Regulation 2016/679 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 27 April 2016 on the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data and on the free movement of such data (‘GDPR’, General Data Protection Regulation), and allow additional data to be added to it.
This Protocol describes the terms and conditions on the data exchange between Supplying and Receiving Data Controllers via PEH Data Platform, the role of the PEH Data Platform Manager, as well as the processing activities performed by the Receiving Data Controller.
The Protocol On the management and use of the Personal Exposure and Health Data Platform can be consulted at doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19232226
To initiate the signature process to sign the Protocol On the management and use of the Personal Exposure and Health Data Platform, send an e-mail to: PEHDataPlatform@vito.be

title: Getting started
text: This page explains what you need in order to start using the PEH Data Platform and what the platform is (and is not) intended to do.

title: Getting started
text: This page explains what you need in order to start using the PEH Data Platform and what the platform is (and is not) intended to do.

title: Getting started
text:
Before you begin
To use the PEH Data Platform, you typically need:

Access to the platform (web-based or local deployment)
Input data structured according to the relevant PEH specifications
The corresponding codebook or metadata template

No installation or programming is required.

title: Getting started
text:
Typical use
In most cases, users will:

Prepare their data following the agreed template

Load the data into the PEH Data Platform

Inspect structure, variables and value domains

Export results or reports for further review

Each of these steps is explained in more detail in the User guide.

title: Getting started
text:
Important boundaries






Warning



The PEH Data Platform: - does not correct or impute data - does not replace study-specific validation procedures - does not assess scientific validity of the data


Users remain responsible for the correctness and interpretation of their data.

title: Getting started
text:
Next steps
Continue with the User guide for a detailed explanation of inputs, outputs and limitations.
